message:
feat: evidence ledger for supervision decisions, registry conformance tests, and GenServer assertion hardening

Evidence Ledger (bd-35iz1):
- Introduce BindingConstraint enum that captures the specific rule
  determining each supervision decision: MonotoneSeverity,
  ExplicitStopStrategy, EscalateStrategy, RestartAllowed,
  WindowExhausted, InsufficientCost, DeadlineTooClose, InsufficientPolls
- Each variant carries the relevant parameters (attempt count, window
  duration, cost quotas, etc.) so tests can assert on the exact
  reasoning chain without inspecting implementation internals
- Implements Display for human-readable constraint descriptions
- EvidenceEntry records bind the constraint to the child ID and
  timestamp for deterministic test assertions

Registry conformance tests (bd-13l06):
- conformance_no_stale_names_after_task_crash: Verifies cleanup_task
  removes all names for a crashed task across multiple regions while
  preserving names from other tasks, with lease obligation resolution
- conformance_no_stale_names_after_region_stop: Verifies cleanup_region
  removes all names for all tasks in a stopped region while preserving
  names in other regions
- conformance_deterministic_winner_on_simultaneous_register: Property
  test ensuring the first registrant always wins on name conflicts
- conformance_lease_abort_on_cancellation: Verifies lease abort
  semantics and re-registration after cancellation
- conformance_trace_event_ordering: Tests trace event ordering
  stability across seeds

GenServer test hardening:
- Replace panic!() with unreachable!() in cancellation test match arms
  for call and cast error paths, since these branches represent
  logically impossible states (the test explicitly triggers cancellation)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
